title: signature: remove pkg fixtures
body:
@mtrmac PTAL I somehow missed this one also

Signed-off-by: Antonio Murdaca runcom@redhat.com

comments.body:
You’re right the `integration` references to `signature/fixtures` have been ugly… this is not quite helping.

Copy&pasting the `signature` fixtures into `integration` would obviously work, and we probably can live with the duplication (a little cross-package drift might even be beneficial in helping test older versions of the data).

Alternatively, a separate `signature-fixtures` subpackage shared between all test users? That would be cleaner than `"../signature"` but there would still be no way to hide this from `godoc` I guess.

> Copy&pasting the signature fixtures into integration would obviously work, and we probably can live with the duplication (a little cross-package drift might even be beneficial in helping test older versions of the data).

That's the point, I don't think it won't hurt anyone by embedding the stuff from `../signature` in that integration test (which is smoke test and should continue to work or break when stuff change). I think that's the only stop where `signature` folder and/or pkg is read/imported so I wouldn't mind copy/pasting the manifest & digest directly in that test. We can change it later if we really need to.

> > (a little cross-package drift might even be beneficial in helping test older versions of the data).
> > That's the point,

I am a bit confused, you seem to agree with me here but then you seem to be suggesting keeping the `../signature` reference; to be clear, I was thinking about something like https://github.com/mtrmac/skopeo/tree/signature-fixtures .

> to be clear, I was thinking about something like https://github.com/mtrmac/skopeo/tree/signature-fixtures .

That's indeed what I was thinking (sorry for the confusion)
